content.text: Issue #267 is now **In Progress**. Now step 1: the `ars-collections` accessors. Let me read the relevant parts of `tree_collection.rs`.

content.content:
595	    /// stable-storage operations on `all_nodes`. For DOM reconciliation
596	    /// or any code that drives the visible iteration exposed via
597	    /// [`Collection::nodes`] / [`Collection::get_by_index`], use
598	    /// [`visible_index_of`](Self::visible_index_of) instead.
599	    pub fn flat_index_of(&self, key: &Key) -> Option<usize> {
600	        self.key_to_index.get(key).copied()
601	    }
602	
603	    /// Returns `true` when the node at `key` currently has at least one
604	    /// child (i.e. it is a branch, not a leaf), or `false` when the
605	    /// node is a leaf **or** the key is unknown.
606	    ///
607	    /// Mirrors the [`Node::has_children`] field. Available without the
608	    /// `T: Clone` bound that `Collection::get(&node).has_children` would
609	    /// require, so callers operating on a generic `T: CollectionItem`
610	    /// (such as [`MutableTreeData`]'s mutation methods) can detect
611	    /// leaf↔branch transitions without paying for `Clone`.
612	    ///
613	    /// [`Node::has_children`]: crate::node::Node::has_children
614	    /// [`MutableTreeData`]: crate::mutable::MutableTreeData
615	    #[must_use]
616	    pub fn has_children(&self, key: &Key) -> bool {
617	        self.key_to_index
618	            .get(key)
619	            .is_some_and(|&i| self.all_nodes[i].has_children)
620	    }
621	
622	    /// Get the **visible** iteration index of a node by key, or `None`
623	    /// when the key is unknown **or** the node sits inside a collapsed
624	    /// ancestor (and is therefore hidden from `Collection::nodes` /
625	    /// `Collection::get_by_index`).
626	    ///
627	    /// Adapters driving DOM reconciliation should consume visible indices
628	    /// — the flat DFS index returned by [`flat_index_of`](Self::flat_index_of)
629	    /// does not correspond to any DOM position when a node is hidden.
630	    pub fn visible_index_of(&self, key: &Key) -> Option<usize> {
631	        let flat = self.flat_index_of(key)?;
632	        // `visible_indices` is sorted ascending (it preserves DFS order),
633	        // so binary search is correct and faster than a linear scan for
634	        // large trees.
635	        self.visible_indices.binary_search(&flat).ok()
636	    }
637	
638	    /// Iterate the **visible** keys of the tree in DFS order.
639	    ///
640	    /// Mirrors the iteration of [`Collection::keys`] but is available
641	    /// without the `T: Clone` bound the trait impl requires, so it can be
642	    /// called from generic contexts (such as `MutableTreeData`'s mutation
643	    /// methods, which only require `T: CollectionItem`).
644	    pub fn visible_keys(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Key> {
645	        self.visible_indices.iter().map(|&i| &self.all_nodes[i].key)
646	    }
647	
648	    /// Move a node (and its subtree) to a new parent at the given child index.
649	    ///

1	//! Accordion navigation component.
2	//!
3	//! Accordion owns the framework-agnostic disclosure-group state: open item
4	//! keys, item registration, disabled guards, roving focus intent, orientation
5	//! aware keyboard navigation, and ARIA/data attributes for the accordion
6	//! anatomy. The agnostic core never moves DOM focus itself; it emits a typed
7	//! [`Effect::FocusFocusedItem`] intent and adapters focus their own native
8	//! element handles.
9	
10	use alloc::{
11	    collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet},
12	    string::String,
13	    vec::Vec,
14	};
15	use core::fmt::{self, Debug};
16	
17	use ars_collections::Key;
18	use ars_core::{
19	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi,
20	    Direction, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Orientation, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan,
21	};
22	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
23	
24	use super::key_token::dom_safe_key_token;
25	
26	/// The only accordion machine state.
27	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
28	pub enum State {
29	    /// Runtime state is held in [`Context`].
30	    #[default]
31	    Idle,
32	}
33	
34	/// Events accepted by the accordion state machine.
35	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
36	pub enum Event {
37	    /// Expand an item by key.
38	    ExpandItem(Key),
39	
40	    /// Collapse an item by key.
41	    CollapseItem(Key),
42	
43	    /// Toggle an item by key.
44	    ToggleItem(Key),
45	
46	    /// Expand every registered enabled item. Ignored in single mode.
47	    ExpandAll,
48	
49	    /// Collapse every open enabled item.
50	    CollapseAll,
51	
52	    /// A trigger received focus.
53	    Focus(Key),
54	
55	    /// Focus left the accordion trigger set.
56	    Blur,
57	
58	    /// Set the resolved text direction used for keyboard navigation.
59	    SetDirection(Direction),
60	
61	    /// Move focus to the next enabled trigger.
62	    FocusNext,
63	
64	    /// Move focus to the previous enabled trigger.
65	    FocusPrev,
66	
67	    /// Move focus to the first enabled trigger.
68	    FocusFirst,
69	
70	    /// Move focus to the last enabled trigger.
71	    FocusLast,
72	
73	    /// Replace the registered item list in DOM order.
74	    SetItems(Vec<ItemRegistration>),
75	
76	    /// Synchronize prop-backed context fields.
77	    SyncProps,
78	
79	    /// Synchronize the controlled open-item set.
80	    SyncControlledValue(BTreeSet<Key>),
81	}
82	
83	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the accordion machine.
84	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
85	pub enum Effect {
86	    /// Adapter must move DOM focus to [`Context::focused_item`].
87	    FocusFocusedItem,
88	}
89	
90	/// Adapter-supplied registration data for one rendered accordion item.

305	    /// Disabled flags keyed by item key.
306	    pub disabled_items: BTreeMap<Key, bool>,
307	
308	    /// Hydration-stable generated ids for accordion parts.
309	    pub ids: ComponentIds,
310	}
311	
312	/// Anatomy parts exposed by the accordion connect API.
313	#[derive(ComponentPart)]
314	#[scope = "accordion"]
315	pub enum Part {
316	    /// The outer accordion root.
317	    Root,
318	
319	    /// An item wrapper.
320	    Item {
321	        /// Item key.
322	        item_key: Key,
323	    },
324	
325	    /// Heading wrapper around an item trigger.
326	    ItemHeader {
327	        /// Item key.
328	        item_key: Key,
329	    },
330	
331	    /// Button that toggles an item.
332	    ItemTrigger {
333	        /// Item key.
334	        item_key: Key,
335	    },
336	
337	    /// Visual indicator inside a trigger.
338	    ItemIndicator {
339	        /// Item key.
340	        item_key: Key,
341	    },
342	
343	    /// Collapsible content panel.
344	    ItemContent {
345	        /// Item key.
346	        item_key: Key,
347	    },
348	}
349	
350	/// State machine for the accordion component.
351	#[derive(Debug)]
352	pub struct Machine;
353	
354	impl ars_core::Machine for Machine {
355	    type State = State;
356	    type Event = Event;
357	    type Context = Context;
358	    type Props = Props;
359	    type Messages = Messages;

1	//! Navigation component machines.
2	//!
3	//! Components in this module help the user move between sections, pages, or
4	//! views: tabbed interfaces, accordions, breadcrumbs, paginators, and so on.
5	
6	mod key_token;
7	
8	/// Breadcrumb navigation component.
9	pub mod breadcrumbs;
10	
11	/// Link navigation component.
12	pub mod link;
13	
14	/// Pagination navigation component.
15	pub mod pagination;
16	
17	/// Steps navigation component.
18	pub mod steps;
19	
20	/// Accordion navigation component.
21	pub mod accordion;
22	
23	/// Tabs component — a tab list paired with associated content panels.
24	pub mod tabs;
25	
26	/// Navigation menu component — menubar navigation with delayed submenu content.
27	pub mod navigation_menu;

1	//! Spec-conformance tests for `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/*`.
2	//!
3	//! Each test pulls the expected anatomy from the corresponding component
4	//! spec's §2 / §5 anatomy tables and asserts the impl's `Part` enum
5	//! matches the declared `(scope, part-name)` ordering.
6	
7	use ars_collections::Key;
8	use ars_components::navigation::{
9	    accordion, breadcrumbs, link, navigation_menu, pagination, steps, tabs,
10	};
11	use ars_core::SafeUrl;
12	
13	use super::helper::assert_anatomy;
14	
15	#[test]
16	fn tabs_anatomy_matches_spec() {
17	    // Spec references:
18	    // - `spec/components/navigation/tabs.md` §2 base anatomy table
19	    //   declares Root / List / Tab / Indicator / Panel.
20	    // - §5.4 anatomy addition declares the Closable variant's
21	    //   `tab-close-trigger` part.
22	    //
23	    // Workspace convention (see `tests/spec_conformance/helper.rs`)
24	    // requires the first variant to be `Root`. The remaining variants
25	    // are listed in spec §2 declaration order, with `TabCloseTrigger`
26	    // appended last because §5 layers it on top of the base anatomy.
27	    assert_anatomy(
28	        "tabs",
29	        &[
30	            (tabs::Part::Root, "root"),
31	            (tabs::Part::List, "list"),
32	            (
33	                tabs::Part::Tab {
34	                    tab_key: Key::default(),
35	                },
36	                "tab",
37	            ),
38	            (tabs::Part::TabIndicator, "tab-indicator"),
39	            (
40	                tabs::Part::Panel {
41	                    tab_key: Key::default(),
42	                    tab_label: None,
43	                },
44	                "panel",
45	            ),
46	            (
47	                tabs::Part::TabCloseTrigger {
48	                    tab_label: String::new(),
49	                },
50	                "tab-close-trigger",
51	            ),
52	        ],
53	    );
54	}
55

58	    assert_anatomy(
59	        "breadcrumbs",
60	        &[

content.text: `KeyboardKey` has no `Character` variant — typed chars come from `data.character: Option<char>`. The tree uses active-descendant (root `tabindex=0` + `aria-activedescendant`), so the focus-nav effect is really scroll-into-view (spec §3.5), which I'll name `Effect::ScrollFocusedIntoView`. Before writing `Props`, I must verify whether `Callback` implements `PartialEq` (Props must derive `PartialEq`).

content.text: `Callback<T>` implements `Clone + Debug + PartialEq` (pointer identity), and checkbox stores `on_checked_change: Option<Callback<...>>` in Props while also emitting `Effect::CheckedChange`. That's my exact model. Let me study checkbox's callback+effect wiring.

content.text: This is the complete pattern set I needed. Now I'll write the full `tree_view/mod.rs`. The tree uses the active-descendant pattern, so I'll generate stable node DOM ids (for `aria-activedescendant`) and emit `Effect::ScrollFocusedIntoView` on focus navigation (spec §3.5), plus `Effect::Reorder` (mirroring checkbox's `Effect::CheckedChange` + `on_*` callback wiring).

attachment.snippet:
1	//! TreeView navigation component.
2	//!
3	//! TreeView owns the framework-agnostic state of a hierarchical, keyboard
4	//! navigable tree implementing the WAI-ARIA tree pattern (`role="tree"`,
5	//! `role="treeitem"`, `role="group"`): expanded/collapsed branch keys, node
6	//! selection (single or multiple), the active-descendant focus indicator,
7	//! typeahead, and the ARIA/`data-ars-*` attributes for every anatomy part.
8	//!
9	//! The data model is a [`TreeCollection<TreeItem>`] from `ars-collections`,
10	//! which owns parent/child relationships and visible-key computation.
11	//!
12	//! The agnostic core never touches the DOM. Focus uses the active-descendant
13	//! pattern (the root keeps `tabindex="0"` and points `aria-activedescendant`
14	//! at the focused node's stable id); when focus moves the core emits a
15	//! [`Effect::ScrollFocusedIntoView`] intent and adapters resolve the live
16	//! element to scroll. The optional drag-and-drop reorder surface
17	//! ([`Props::dnd_enabled`]) owns all pure data-model logic — valid drop-slot
18	//! enumeration, cycle-free drop validation, and [`ReorderEvent`] path
19	//! computation — and surfaces completion through [`Props::on_reorder`] driven
20	//! by a named [`Effect::Reorder`]. Pointer hit-testing, hover-expand timing,
21	//! and `use_drag`/`use_drop` wiring remain adapter concerns.
22	
23	use alloc::{
24	    collections::BTreeSet,
25	    string::{String, ToString},
26	    vec::Vec,
27	};
28	use core::fmt::{self, Debug};
29	
30	use ars_collections::{
31	    Collection, Key, Node, TreeCollection,
32	    dnd::{CollectionDropTarget, DropPosition},
33	    selection,
34	};
35	use ars_core::{
36	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart,
37	    ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan,
38	    no_cleanup,
39	};
40	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
41	
42	use super::key_token::dom_safe_key_token;
43	
44	/// Value type stored in the [`TreeCollection`] backing a [`TreeView`](self).
45	///
46	/// Carries the per-node properties the machine needs that are not part of the
47	/// collection's structural model: the display label, disabled state, the
48	/// lazy-load `has_children` affordance, and an optional navigation `href`.
49	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
50	pub struct TreeItem {
51	    /// Display label for the node (used in typeahead and accessibility).
52	    pub label: String,
53	
54	    /// Whether this node is disabled (not selectable, skipped during selection).
55	    pub disabled: bool,
56	
57	    /// When `true`, the node renders an expand affordance even if it has no
58	    /// children loaded yet. Essential for lazy-loaded trees where the child
59	    /// count is unknown until the node is first expanded.
60	    pub has_children: bool,
61	
62	    /// Optional navigation URL. When `Some`, the clickable area renders as an
63	    /// `<a>` element (native middle-click / Ctrl+click), while the parent
64	    /// `role="treeitem"` is preserved.
65	    pub href: Option<String>,
66	}
67	
68	impl Default for TreeItem {
69	    fn default() -> Self {
70	        Self {
71	            label: String::new(),
72	            disabled: false,
73	            has_children: false,
74	            href: None,
75	        }
76	    }
77	}
78	
79	/// Machine states for [`TreeView`](self).
80	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
81	pub enum State {
82	    /// The tree does not have keyboard focus.
83	    #[default]
84	    Idle,
85	
86	    /// The tree has keyboard focus on some node.
87	    Focused,
88	}
89	
90	/// Events accepted by the [`TreeView`](self) state machine.
91	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
92	pub enum Event {
93	    /// Expand a branch node.
94	    ExpandNode(Key),
95	
96	    /// Collapse a branch node.
97	    CollapseNode(Key),
98	
99	    /// Toggle the expand/collapse state of a branch node.
100	    ToggleNode(Key),
101	
102	    /// Add a node to the selection (respecting selection mode/behavior).
103	    SelectNode(Key),
104	
105	    /// Remove a node from the selection.
106	    DeselectNode(Key),
107	
108	    /// Move the focus indicator to a specific node.
109	    FocusNode(Key),
110	
111	    /// Move focus to the next visible node (wraps to the first).
112	    FocusNext,
113	
114	    /// Move focus to the previous visible node (wraps to the last).
115	    FocusPrev,
116	
117	    /// Move focus to the first visible node.
118	    FocusFirst,
119	
120	    /// Move focus to the last visible node.
121	    FocusLast,
122	
123	    /// Move focus to the parent of the focused node.
124	    FocusParent,
125	
126	    /// The tree container received focus.
127	    Focus {
128	        /// Whether focus arrived via the keyboard.
129	        is_keyboard: bool,
130	    },
131	
132	    /// The tree container lost focus.
133	    Blur,
134	
135	    /// Jump to the next visible node whose label starts with the character.
136	    TypeaheadSearch(char),
137	
138	    /// Expand every expandable node in the tree.
139	    ExpandAll,
140	
141	    /// Collapse every node in the tree.
142	    CollapseAll,
143	
144	    /// Begin a drag (keyboard pickup or pointer drag start) on a node.
145	    /// Ignored unless [`Props::dnd_enabled`].
146	    DragStart(Key),
147	
148	    /// Set the resolved drop target (adapter pointer hit-testing). Ignored when
149	    /// no drag is active or the target is invalid.
150	    DragOver(CollectionDropTarget),
151	
152	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the next valid slot.
153	    DragMoveNext,
154	
155	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the previous valid slot.
156	    DragMovePrev,
157	
158	    /// Confirm the drop at the current drop target (fires [`Props::on_reorder`]).
159	    Drop,
160	
161	    /// Cancel the active drag and discard the drop target.
162	    CancelDrag,
163	}
164	
165	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the [`TreeView`](self) machine.
166	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
167	pub enum Effect {
168	    /// Adapter must scroll the active-descendant node ([`Context::focused_node`])
169	    /// into view. The agnostic core never moves DOM focus itself; the tree uses
170	    /// the active-descendant pattern, so only scrolling is adapter-resolved.
171	    ScrollFocusedIntoView,
172	
173	    /// Adapter invokes [`Props::on_reorder`] with the completed [`ReorderEvent`].
174	    Reorder,
175	}
176	
177	impl ars_core::EffectName for Effect {}
178	
179	/// Emitted when a tree node is reordered via drag-and-drop.
180	///
181	/// Paths are sequences of keys from the root to the item, so consumers can
182	/// apply the reorder against their hierarchical data source without resolving
183	/// flat indices.
184	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
185	pub struct ReorderEvent {
186	    /// Path of the dragged node (root → node).
187	    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
188	
189	    /// Path of the drop target node (root → target).
190	    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
191	
192	    /// Where, relative to the target, the node was dropped.
193	    pub position: DropPosition,
194	}
195	
196	/// Localizable messages for [`TreeView`](self).
197	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
198	pub struct Messages {
199	    /// Loading indicator text for branches whose children are being fetched
200	    /// (default: `"Loading…"`). Rendered as visually hidden text and announced
201	    /// via `aria-live="polite"` by adapters.
202	    pub loading_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
203	
204	    /// Accessible label template for a node's drag handle, called with the
205	    /// node's label (default: `"Drag {label}"`).
206	    pub drag_handle_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
207	}
208	
209	impl Default for Messages {
210	    fn default() -> Self {
211	        Self {
212	            loading_label: MessageFn::static_str("Loading\u{2026}"),
213	            drag_handle_label: MessageFn::new(|label: &str, _locale: &Locale| {
214	                alloc::format!("Drag {label}")
215	            }),
216	        }
217	    }
218	}
219	
220	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
221

1 //! Shared helpers for spec-conformance tests. 2 //! 3 //! Per-component tests call [`assert_anatomy`] with the expected scope and 4 //! the expected `(part, kebab-name)` list. The helper checks every facet 5 //! of the `ComponentPart` contract that the spec's §3 anatomy table 6 //! declares: 7 //! 8 //! 1. `Part::scope()` matches the spec's `data-ars-scope` token. 9 //! 2. `Part::all()` produces exactly the declared parts in the declared 10 //! order. 11 //! 3. Every declared part round-trips through `Part::name()` with the 12 //! declared kebab-case token. 13 //! 4. `Part::ROOT` equals the first declared part (workspace convention: 14 //! every `Part` enum's first variant is `Root`). 15 16 use ars_core::ComponentPart; 17 18 /// Asserts that the supplied `Part` enum matches the spec's anatomy 19 /// declaration. 20 /// 21 /// `expected_scope` is the `#[scope = "..."]` token the spec puts on the 22 /// `data-ars-scope` attribute. `expected_parts` is the ordered list of 23 /// `(part, kebab-name)` pairs from the spec's §3 anatomy table. 24 pub(crate) fn assert_anatomy

(expected_scope: &'static str, expected_parts: &[(P, &'static str)]) 25 where 26 P: ComponentPart + core::fmt::Debug + PartialEq, 27 { 28 assert_eq!( 29 P::scope(), 30 expected_scope, 31 "Part::scope() must match spec §3 anatomy", 32 ); 33 34 let actual = P::all(); 35 36 let actual_names = actual.iter().map(ComponentPart::name).collect::>(); 37 38 let expected_names = expected_parts 39 .iter() 40 .map(|(_, name)| *name) 41 .collect::>(); 42 43 assert_eq!( 44 actual_names, expected_names, 45 "Part::all() name list must equal spec §3 anatomy column \ 46 (scope = {expected_scope:?})", 47 ); 48 49 assert_eq!( 50 actual.len(), 51 expected_parts.len(), 52 "Part::all() length must match the number of spec §3 anatomy rows", 53 ); 54 55 for (actual_part, (expected_part, expected_name)) in actual.iter().zip(expected_parts) { 56 assert_eq!( 57 actual_part.name(), 58 *expected_name, 59 "Part {actual_part:?} must produce kebab-name {expected_name:?}", 60 ); 61 assert_eq!( 62 actual_part, expected_part, 63 "Part::all()[i] must equal the expected part value", 64 ); 65 } 66 67 if let Some((first_expected, _)) = expected_parts.first() { 68 assert_eq!( 69 &P::ROOT, 70 first_expected, 71 "Workspace convention: Part::ROOT (the first variant) must \ 72 equal the first spec-declared part", 73 ); 74 } 75 } 76

content.text: Anatomy conformance passes. Before writing the full DnD reorder-callback test, let me confirm how `Service::send` surfaces effects and whether the effect setup closure (which invokes the callback) runs in unit tests.

`PendingEffect::run(ctx, props, send)` executes the setup closure (invoking the callback), and `send` returns `SendResult { pending_effects, state_changed, ... }`. I have everything. Let me write the comprehensive test suite.

lines: -//! Unit, snapshot, and behavior tests for the `tree_view` machine.
+//! Unit, snapshot, keyboard, and drag-and-drop tests for the `tree_view`
+//! machine. Mirrors the spec's "tests to add first" plus snapshot coverage of
+//! every anatomy part across its output-affecting state/prop/context branches.
+use ars_core::{Env, KeyboardKey, Machine as _, Service, StrongSend};
-use super::{Context, Event, Machine, Messages, Props, TreeItem};
+use super::{Effect, Event, Machine, Messages, Props, ReorderEvent, TreeItem, snapshot_attrs};
+fn leaf_with(key: u64, label: &str, value: TreeItem) -> TreeItemConfig<TreeItem> {
-// Silence unused-import warnings until the full suite lands.
+    // Regression: the selection state machine must agree with the `selected`
+    // binding at init, otherwise the first DeselectNode would operate on an
+    // empty state and lose the seeded selection.
+    // Both expandable branches (Fruits, Vegetables) must be expanded, even
+    // though Vegetables started collapsed (its descendants were hidden).
+    // Carrot/Daikon are hidden; next visible after Vegetables is Grains.
+    // Carrot (5) is hidden under collapsed Vegetables; typing 'c' must not jump
+    // Apple is child 1 of 2 under Fruits.
+    // Root level has 3 nodes (Fruits, Vegetables, Grains).
+    // Vegetables (4) is a collapsed branch -> expand it.
+    // Fruits (1) is already expanded -> enter (move to next visible).
+    // Vegetables (4) is collapsed -> move to parent.
+    // Focused on Fruits (root level); '*' expands all expandable root siblings:
+    // Fruits (1) and Vegetables (4) — Grains (7) is a leaf.
+    // Onto a descendant (Apple) -> rejected (would create a cycle).
+    // First valid slot: target 1 (Fruits), Before. (2 excluded as dragged.)
+    // Prev from the first slot wraps to the last valid slot: target 7, After.
+    // The core never mutates its collection on drop — the consumer applies it.
+    // Running the effect invokes `on_reorder` with the resolved paths.
+    // Not dragging yet: Enter picks up.
+    // Escape cancels; arrows step the target.
+// Snapshots — every anatomy part across output-affecting branches
+    // Lazy leaf: has_children flag set but no real children -> aria-expanded.

content.text: Most tests pass. Three logic failures share one root cause: the component's `ctx.expanded` is seeded only from `props.default_expanded` (empty in my tests), ignoring the collection's per-node `default_expanded` (Fruits). That makes a `TreeItemConfig { default_expanded: true }` silently non-expanded — a pit-of-failure. I'll seed initial expansion from the collection's default-expanded nodes too (union with `props.default_expanded`) for the uncontrolled case. Let me check `Node.is_expanded` is the right signal, then fix `init`.

1	//! Property-based tests for the navigation/tabs state machine.
2	//!
3	//! Each `proptest!` block is `#[ignore]`d so the default `cargo test`
4	//! run skips them; the nightly `extended-proptest` job clears the
5	//! ignore filter and runs them with a higher case count via
6	//! `PROPTEST_CASES`.
7	
8	use std::collections::BTreeSet;
9	
10	use ars_collections::Key;
11	use ars_components::navigation::{
12	    accordion, pagination, steps,
13	    tabs::{ActivationMode, Effect, Event, Machine, Messages, Props, State, TabRegistration},
14	};
15	use ars_core::{AriaAttr, Direction, Env, HtmlAttr, Machine as MachineTrait, Orientation, Service};
16	use proptest::{prelude::*, test_runner::TestCaseResult};
17	
18	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19	// Strategies
20	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21	
22	fn arb_key() -> impl Strategy<Value = Key> {
23	    // Small key universe so collision/registration paths exercise.
24	    prop_oneof![
25	        Just(Key::str("a")),
26	        Just(Key::str("b")),
27	        Just(Key::str("c")),
28	        Just(Key::str("d")),
29	        Just(Key::Int(0)),
30	        Just(Key::Int(1)),
31	    ]
32	}
33	
34	fn arb_orientation() -> impl Strategy<Value = Orientation> {
35	    prop_oneof![Just(Orientation::Horizontal), Just(Orientation::Vertical)]
36	}
37	
38	fn arb_activation_mode() -> impl Strategy<Value = ActivationMode> {
39	    prop_oneof![
40	        Just(ActivationMode::Automatic),
41	        Just(ActivationMode::Manual),
42	    ]
43	}
44	
45	fn arb_direction() -> impl Strategy<Value = Direction> {
46	    prop_oneof![
47	        Just(Direction::Ltr),
48	        Just(Direction::Rtl),
49	        Just(Direction::Auto),
50	    ]
51	}
52	
53	fn arb_disabled_keys() -> impl Strategy<Value = BTreeSet<Key>> {
54	    prop::collection::vec(arb_key(), 0..3).prop_map(|keys| keys.into_iter().collect())
55	}
56	
57	fn arb_props() -> impl Strategy<Value = Props> {
58	    (
59	        prop::option::of(prop::option::of(arb_key())),
60	        prop::option::of(arb_key()),

attachment.snippet:
1	//! TreeView navigation component.
2	//!
3	//! TreeView owns the framework-agnostic state of a hierarchical, keyboard
4	//! navigable tree implementing the WAI-ARIA tree pattern (`role="tree"`,
5	//! `role="treeitem"`, `role="group"`): expanded/collapsed branch keys, node
6	//! selection (single or multiple), the active-descendant focus indicator,
7	//! typeahead, and the ARIA/`data-ars-*` attributes for every anatomy part.
8	//!
9	//! The data model is a [`TreeCollection<TreeItem>`] from `ars-collections`,
10	//! which owns parent/child relationships and visible-key computation.
11	//!
12	//! The agnostic core never touches the DOM. Focus uses the active-descendant
13	//! pattern (the root keeps `tabindex="0"` and points `aria-activedescendant`
14	//! at the focused node's stable id); when focus moves the core emits a
15	//! [`Effect::ScrollFocusedIntoView`] intent and adapters resolve the live
16	//! element to scroll. The optional drag-and-drop reorder surface
17	//! ([`Props::dnd_enabled`]) owns all pure data-model logic — valid drop-slot
18	//! enumeration, cycle-free drop validation, and [`ReorderEvent`] path
19	//! computation — and surfaces completion through [`Props::on_reorder`] driven
20	//! by a named [`Effect::Reorder`]. Pointer hit-testing, hover-expand timing,
21	//! and `use_drag`/`use_drop` wiring remain adapter concerns.
22	
23	use alloc::{
24	    collections::BTreeSet,
25	    string::{String, ToString},
26	    vec::Vec,
27	};
28	use core::fmt::{self, Debug};
29	
30	use ars_collections::{
31	    Collection, Key, Node, TreeCollection,
32	    dnd::{CollectionDropTarget, DropPosition},
33	    selection,
34	};
35	use ars_core::{
36	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart,
37	    ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan,
38	    no_cleanup,
39	};
40	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
41	
42	use super::key_token::dom_safe_key_token;
43	
44	/// Value type stored in the [`TreeCollection`] backing a [`TreeView`](self).
45	///
46	/// Carries the per-node properties the machine needs that are not part of the
47	/// collection's structural model: the display label, disabled state, the
48	/// lazy-load `has_children` affordance, and an optional navigation `href`.
49	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
50	pub struct TreeItem {
51	    /// Display label for the node (used in typeahead and accessibility).
52	    pub label: String,
53	
54	    /// Whether this node is disabled (not selectable, skipped during selection).
55	    pub disabled: bool,
56	
57	    /// When `true`, the node renders an expand affordance even if it has no
58	    /// children loaded yet. Essential for lazy-loaded trees where the child
59	    /// count is unknown until the node is first expanded.
60	    pub has_children: bool,
61	
62	    /// Optional navigation URL. When `Some`, the clickable area renders as an
63	    /// `<a>` element (native middle-click / Ctrl+click), while the parent
64	    /// `role="treeitem"` is preserved.
65	    pub href: Option<String>,
66	}
67	
68	/// Machine states for [`TreeView`](self).
69	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
70	pub enum State {
71	    /// The tree does not have keyboard focus.
72	    #[default]
73	    Idle,
74	
75	    /// The tree has keyboard focus on some node.
76	    Focused,
77	}
78	
79	/// Events accepted by the [`TreeView`](self) state machine.
80	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
81	pub enum Event {
82	    /// Expand a branch node.
83	    ExpandNode(Key),
84	
85	    /// Collapse a branch node.
86	    CollapseNode(Key),
87	
88	    /// Toggle the expand/collapse state of a branch node.
89	    ToggleNode(Key),
90	
91	    /// Add a node to the selection (respecting selection mode/behavior).
92	    SelectNode(Key),
93	
94	    /// Remove a node from the selection.
95	    DeselectNode(Key),
96	
97	    /// Move the focus indicator to a specific node.
98	    FocusNode(Key),
99	
100	    /// Move focus to the next visible node (wraps to the first).
101	    FocusNext,
102	
103	    /// Move focus to the previous visible node (wraps to the last).
104	    FocusPrev,
105	
106	    /// Move focus to the first visible node.
107	    FocusFirst,
108	
109	    /// Move focus to the last visible node.
110	    FocusLast,
111	
112	    /// Move focus to the parent of the focused node.
113	    FocusParent,
114	
115	    /// The tree container received focus.
116	    Focus {
117	        /// Whether focus arrived via the keyboard.
118	        is_keyboard: bool,
119	    },
120	
121	    /// The tree container lost focus.
122	    Blur,
123	
124	    /// Jump to the next visible node whose label starts with the character.
125	    TypeaheadSearch(char),
126	
127	    /// Expand every expandable node in the tree.
128	    ExpandAll,
129	
130	    /// Collapse every node in the tree.
131	    CollapseAll,
132	
133	    /// Begin a drag (keyboard pickup or pointer drag start) on a node.
134	    /// Ignored unless [`Props::dnd_enabled`].
135	    DragStart(Key),
136	
137	    /// Set the resolved drop target (adapter pointer hit-testing). Ignored when
138	    /// no drag is active or the target is invalid.
139	    DragOver(CollectionDropTarget),
140	
141	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the next valid slot.
142	    DragMoveNext,
143	
144	    /// Step the keyboard drop target to the previous valid slot.
145	    DragMovePrev,
146	
147	    /// Confirm the drop at the current drop target (fires [`Props::on_reorder`]).
148	    Drop,
149	
150	    /// Cancel the active drag and discard the drop target.
151	    CancelDrag,
152	}
153	
154	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the [`TreeView`](self) machine.
155	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
156	pub enum Effect {
157	    /// Adapter must scroll the active-descendant node ([`Context::focused_node`])
158	    /// into view. The agnostic core never moves DOM focus itself; the tree uses
159	    /// the active-descendant pattern, so only scrolling is adapter-resolved.
160	    ScrollFocusedIntoView,
161	
162	    /// Adapter invokes [`Props::on_reorder`] with the completed [`ReorderEvent`].
163	    Reorder,
164	}
165	
166	/// Emitted when a tree node is reordered via drag-and-drop.
167	///
168	/// Paths are sequences of keys from the root to the item, so consumers can
169	/// apply the reorder against their hierarchical data source without resolving
170	/// flat indices.
171	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
172	pub struct ReorderEvent {
173	    /// Path of the dragged node (root → node).
174	    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
175	
176	    /// Path of the drop target node (root → target).
177	    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
178	
179	    /// Where, relative to the target, the node was dropped.
180	    pub position: DropPosition,
181	}
182	
183	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::loading_label`].
184	pub type LoadingLabelFn = dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
185	
186	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::drag_handle_label`]. Receives the
187	/// node's label and the active locale.
188	pub type DragHandleLabelFn = dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
189	
190	/// Localizable messages for [`TreeView`](self).
191	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
192	pub struct Messages {
193	    /// Loading indicator text for branches whose children are being fetched
194	    /// (default: `"Loading…"`). Rendered as visually hidden text and announced
195	    /// via `aria-live="polite"` by adapters.
196	    pub loading_label: MessageFn<LoadingLabelFn>,
197	
198	    /// Accessible label template for a node's drag handle, called with the
199	    /// node's label (default: `"Drag {label}"`).
200	    pub drag_handle_label: MessageFn<DragHandleLabelFn>,
201	}
202	
203	impl Default for Messages {
204	    fn default() -> Self {
205	        Self {
206	            loading_label: MessageFn::static_str("Loading\u{2026}"),
207	            drag_handle_label: MessageFn::new(|label: &str, _locale: &Locale| {
208	                alloc::format!("Drag {label}")
209	            }),
210	        }
211	    }
212	}
213	
214	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
215

| Event                       | Payload                | Description                                                    |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ExpandNode(Key)`           | node key               | Expand a branch node.                                          |
| `CollapseNode(Key)`         | node key               | Collapse a branch node.                                        |
| `ToggleNode(Key)`           | node key               | Toggle expand/collapse of a branch.                            |
| `SelectNode(Key)`           | node key               | Add a node to the selection.                                   |
| `DeselectNode(Key)`         | node key               | Remove a node from the selection.                              |
| `FocusNode(Key)`            | node key               | Move focus indicator to a node.                                |
| `FocusNext`                 | —                      | Move focus to the next visible node.                           |
| `FocusPrev`                 | —                      | Move focus to the previous visible node.                       |
| `FocusFirst`                | —                      | Move focus to the first visible node.                          |
| `FocusLast`                 | —                      | Move focus to the last visible node.                           |
| `FocusParent`               | —                      | Move focus to the parent of the focused node.                  |
| `Focus { is_keyboard }`     | `bool`                 | The tree container received focus.                             |
| `Blur`                      | —                      | The tree container lost focus.                                 |
| `TypeaheadSearch(char)`     | `char`                 | Jump to the next node whose label starts with the character.   |
| `ExpandAll`                 | —                      | Expand every expandable node in the tree.                      |
| `CollapseAll`               | —                      | Collapse every node in the tree.                               |
| `DragStart(Key)`            | node key               | Begin a drag on a node (drag-and-drop variant, §4).            |
| `DragOver(CollectionDropTarget)` | resolved target   | Set the drop target (adapter pointer hit-testing).             |
| `DragMoveNext` / `DragMovePrev` | —                  | Step the keyboard drop target through valid slots.             |
| `Drop`                      | —                      | Confirm the drop at the current target (fires `on_reorder`).   |
| `CancelDrag`                | —                      | Cancel the active drag.                                        |

content.content:
84	/// Context for the `TreeView` component.
85	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
86	pub struct Context {
87	    /// The tree collection — holds all nodes, parent-child relationships,
88	    /// and visible-key computation. See `06-collections.md §2.3`.
89	    pub items: TreeCollection<TreeItem>,
90	    /// Currently selected node IDs (uses canonical `selection::Set` from `ars_collections`).
91	    pub selected: Bindable<selection::Set>,
92	    /// Full selection state machine (mode, behavior, anchor, focus, disabled keys).
93	    pub selection_state: selection::State,
94	    /// Currently expanded branch node IDs (expansion ≠ selection).
95	    /// Uses `Bindable` for controlled/uncontrolled pattern.
96	    pub expanded: Bindable<BTreeSet<Key>>,
97	    /// The node ID that holds the focus indicator.
98	    pub focused_node: Option<Key>,
99	    /// True when focus arrived via keyboard.
100	    pub focus_visible: bool,
101	    /// Allow multiple nodes to be selected simultaneously.
102	    pub multiple: bool,
103	    /// Controls which nodes can be selected (canonical `ars_collections::selection::Mode`).
104	    pub selection_mode: selection::Mode,
105	    /// Typeahead buffer (for multi-character typeahead, cleared after timeout).
106	    pub typeahead_buffer: String,
107	    /// Resolved locale for i18n.
108	    pub locale: Locale,
109	    /// Resolved translatable messages.
110	    pub messages: Messages,
111	    /// Generated element IDs for all parts.
112	    pub ids: ComponentIds,

-> **Drag and Drop Reorder**: When `dnd_enabled: true` is set, TreeView integrates with `DraggableCollection<T>` and `DroppableCollection<T>` from `06-collections.md` §7. This adds optional `drag::Handle` and `drop::Indicator` anatomy parts. TreeView supports both `CollectionDndEvent::Reorder` (reordering siblings) and `CollectionDndEvent::Move` (reparenting nodes by dropping onto a parent item with `drop::Position::On`).

+> **Drag and Drop Reorder**: When `dnd_enabled: true` is set, TreeView surfaces the agnostic drag-and-drop reorder logic — the `DragHandle` and `DropIndicator` anatomy parts (canonical bare names, per `06-collections.md` §10.8 and the listbox/gridlist/table specs), the drag-state events, valid drop-slot enumeration, cycle-free drop validation, and `ReorderEvent` path computation. It reports completion through `Props::on_reorder` driven by the named `Effect::Reorder`. Dropping **between** siblings uses `DropPosition::Before`/`After`; dropping **onto** a node to reparent uses `DropPosition::On`. The collection-level `DraggableCollection<T>` / `DroppableCollection<T>` trait impls (which bundle the live `selection::State`), pointer hit-testing, the 500 ms hover-expand timer, and `use_drag`/`use_drop` DOM wiring are **adapter** concerns (tracked by the TreeView Leptos/Dioxus adapter tasks), not part of this agnostic core.

1090	```
1091	
1092	> **Drag and Drop Reorder**: When `dnd_enabled: true` is set, TreeView surfaces the agnostic drag-and-drop reorder logic — the `DragHandle` and `DropIndicator` anatomy parts (canonical bare names, per `06-collections.md` §10.8 and the listbox/gridlist/table specs), the drag-state events, valid drop-slot enumeration, cycle-free drop validation, and `ReorderEvent` path computation. It reports completion through `Props::on_reorder` driven by the named `Effect::Reorder`. Dropping **between** siblings uses `DropPosition::Before`/`After`; dropping **onto** a node to reparent uses `DropPosition::On`. The collection-level `DraggableCollection<T>` / `DroppableCollection<T>` trait impls (which bundle the live `selection::State`), pointer hit-testing, the 500 ms hover-expand timer, and `use_drag`/`use_drop` DOM wiring are **adapter** concerns (tracked by the TreeView Leptos/Dioxus adapter tasks), not part of this agnostic core.
1093	
1094	### 4.2 Drag-Reorder Behavior
1095	
1096	When drag-and-drop reorder is enabled (`dnd_enabled: true`), the following behaviors apply:
1097	
1098	- **Drag handle**: Each tree item renders an optional drag handle affordance (the `drag::Handle` anatomy part). The handle is the grab target; the entire row is not draggable by default to avoid conflicts with text selection.
1099	- **Hover-expand**: When dragging over a collapsed tree node, the node automatically expands after a 500ms hover delay. This allows users to drop items into nested levels without manually expanding first.
1100	- **Drop target indication**: Valid drop zones are visually highlighted during drag. Three drop positions are supported:
1101	  - `Before` — insert before the target node (horizontal line above)
1102	  - `After` — insert after the target node (horizontal line below)
1103	  - `Child` — insert as a child of the target node (target node background highlight)
1104	- **Drop position detection**: The drop position is determined by the pointer's vertical position within the target row: top 25% = `Before`, bottom 25% = `After`, middle 50% = `Child`.
1105	- **Keyboard drag**: Full keyboard support for reordering:
1106	  - `Enter` or `Space` on a focused drag handle to pick up the item
1107	  - Arrow keys to move the item through valid drop positions
1108	  - `Enter` to confirm the drop
1109	  - `Escape` to cancel and return the item to its original position
1110	- **Reorder callback**: `on_reorder: Callback<ReorderEvent>` fires when a drop is completed.
1111	
1112	```rust
1113	/// Event emitted when a tree item is reordered via drag-and-drop.
1114	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
1115	pub struct ReorderEvent {
1116	    /// Path of the dragged item (sequence of keys from root to item).
1117	    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
1118	    /// Path of the drop target.
1119	    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
1120	    /// Where relative to the target the item was dropped.
1121	    pub position: DropPosition,
1122	}
1123	
1124	// `DropPosition` — defined in `06-collections.md`
1125	```
1126	
1127	## 5. Variant: Lazy Loading
1128	
1129	`TreeView` supports lazy loading of child nodes for large or server-driven hierarchies. When a node is first expanded and its children are not yet loaded, the machine fires a `LoadChildren` event.
1130	
1131	### 5.1 Additional Props
1132	
1133	```rust,no_check
1134	/// The load_children callback is registered in the adapter layer, not in Props.
1135	/// When provided, nodes without pre-loaded children will trigger lazy loading on expand.
1136	/// The adapter observes `LoadChildren(Key)` events and calls the user-provided callback.
1137	```
1138	
1139	```rust
1140	/// Type alias for the lazy-load callback.
1141	/// The adapter calls this with the parent node's key when children need loading.
1142	/// The callback should eventually send a `ChildrenLoaded` event back to the machine.
1143	/// Uses `Rc` so the adapter can clone it into event handler closures.
1144	pub type LoadChildrenFn = Rc<dyn Fn(Key)>;
1145	```
1146	
1147	### 5.2 Additional Events
1148	
1149	```rust,no_check
1150	/// Added to TreeView Event enum.
1151	/// Fired when a node is expanded for the first time and has no children loaded.

input.old_string:
When drag-and-drop reorder is enabled (`dnd_enabled: true`), the following behaviors apply:

- **Drag handle**: Each tree item renders an optional drag handle affordance (the `drag::Handle` anatomy part). The handle is the grab target; the entire row is not draggable by default to avoid conflicts with text selection.
- **Hover-expand**: When dragging over a collapsed tree node, the node automatically expands after a 500ms hover delay. This allows users to drop items into nested levels without manually expanding first.
- **Drop target indication**: Valid drop zones are visually highlighted during drag. Three drop positions are supported:
  - `Before` — insert before the target node (horizontal line above)
  - `After` — insert after the target node (horizontal line below)
  - `Child` — insert as a child of the target node (target node background highlight)
- **Drop position detection**: The drop position is determined by the pointer's vertical position within the target row: top 25% = `Before`, bottom 25% = `After`, middle 50% = `Child`.
- **Keyboard drag**: Full keyboard support for reordering:
  - `Enter` or `Space` on a focused drag handle to pick up the item
  - Arrow keys to move the item through valid drop positions
  - `Enter` to confirm the drop
  - `Escape` to cancel and return the item to its original position
- **Reorder callback**: `on_reorder: Callback<ReorderEvent>` fires when a drop is completed.

```rust
/// Event emitted when a tree item is reordered via drag-and-drop.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct ReorderEvent {
    /// Path of the dragged item (sequence of keys from root to item).
    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
    /// Path of the drop target.
    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
    /// Where relative to the target the item was dropped.
    pub position: DropPosition,
}

// `DropPosition` — defined in `06-collections.md`
```
input.new_string:
When drag-and-drop reorder is enabled (`dnd_enabled: true`), the following behaviors apply. The
**agnostic core** owns the data-model logic (slot enumeration, validity, paths, attrs, keyboard
protocol); pointer hit-testing and the hover-expand timer are **adapter**-resolved.

- **Drag handle**: Each tree item renders an optional drag handle affordance (the `DragHandle`
  anatomy part). The handle is the grab target; the entire row is not draggable by default to
  avoid conflicts with text selection. `drag_handle_attrs` emits `role="button"` and a localized
  `aria-label` from `Messages::drag_handle_label`, plus `aria-grabbed="true"` while dragging.
- **Hover-expand** (adapter): When the pointer dwells over a collapsed node during a drag, the
  adapter expands it after a 500 ms hover delay by sending `ExpandNode`. The agnostic core has no
  timer.
- **Drop target indication**: The `DropIndicator` part marks the current drop slot with
  `data-ars-drop-position` (`before` / `on` / `after`) and `data-ars-drop-target` (the target
  node's id). Three drop positions are supported:
  - `DropPosition::Before` — insert before the target node (horizontal line above)
  - `DropPosition::After` — insert after the target node (horizontal line below)
  - `DropPosition::On` — reparent as a child of the target node (target background highlight)
- **Drop position detection** (adapter): The adapter maps the pointer's vertical position within
  the target row — top 25% = `Before`, bottom 25% = `After`, middle 50% = `On` — to a
  `CollectionDropTarget` and sends `DragOver`.
- **Keyboard drag** (agnostic core, via `on_drag_handle_keydown`):
  - `Enter` / `Space` on a focused drag handle picks up the item (`DragStart`).
  - Arrow Up/Down step the item through the valid drop slots (`DragMovePrev` / `DragMoveNext`).
  - `Enter` confirms the drop (`Drop`).
  - `Escape` cancels and discards the drop target (`CancelDrag`).
- **Drop validity**: The core rejects dropping a node onto itself or any of its descendants
  (cycle prevention) in both `DragOver` and `Drop`.
- **Reorder callback**: `Props::on_reorder: Option<Callback<ReorderEvent>>` fires when a drop is
  completed. The core never mutates `ctx.items`; the consumer applies the reorder to its data
  source and re-supplies props (pure-notification, like Tabs).

```rust
/// Event emitted when a tree node is reordered via drag-and-drop.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct ReorderEvent {
    /// Path of the dragged node (sequence of keys from root to node).
    pub source_path: Vec<Key>,
    /// Path of the drop target.
    pub target_path: Vec<Key>,
    /// Where relative to the target the node was dropped.
    pub position: DropPosition,
}

// `DropPosition` and `CollectionDropTarget` — defined in `ars_collections::dnd`
// (`06-collections.md` §10). Reconciliation: `CollectionDndEvent::Reorder`/`Move`
// is the low-level *collection* contract (flat `keys` + a single `target`);
// `ReorderEvent` is the TreeView *component* surface (hierarchical root→node
// paths). The adapter maps between them.
```

949	
950	```text
951	TreeView
952	└── Root                           role="tree"
953	    ├── Branch                     role="treeitem" aria-expanded
954	    │   ├── BranchControl        (clickable row; <a> when href present)
955	    │   │   ├── BranchIndicator  aria-hidden="true"
956	    │   │   └── BranchText
957	    │   └── BranchContent        role="group"
958	    │       └── (nested Branch or Leaf nodes)
959	    └── Leaf                       role="treeitem" (aria-expanded when has_children)

961	```
962	
963	| Part              | Element                               | Key Attributes                                                                                                                                                                                              |
964	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
965	| `Root`            | `<ul>` or `<div>`                     | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="root"`, `role="tree"`, `aria-multiselectable`                                                                                                                 |
966	| `Branch`          | `<li>` or `<div>`                     | `role="treeitem"`, `aria-expanded` (also set when `has_children` is true with no loaded children), `aria-selected`, `aria-level`, `aria-setsize`, `aria-posinset`, `data-ars-expanded`, `data-ars-selected` |

968	| `BranchIndicator` | `<span>`                              | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="branch-indicator"`, `aria-hidden="true"`, `data-ars-expanded`                                                                                                 |
969	| `BranchText`      | `<span>`                              | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="branch-text"`                                                                                                                                                 |
970	| `BranchContent`   | `<ul>` or `<div>`                     | `role="group"`, `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="branch-content"`                                                                                                                              |
971	| `Leaf`            | `<li>`, `<div>`, or `<a>` (when href) | `role="treeitem"`, `aria-expanded="false"` (when `has_children` is true), `aria-selected`, `aria-level`, `aria-setsize`, `aria-posinset`, `data-ars-selected`, `href` (when present)                        |

file.content:
## 2. Anatomy

```text
TreeView
└── Root                           role="tree"
    ├── Branch                     role="treeitem" aria-expanded
    │   ├── BranchControl        (clickable row; <a> when href present)
    │   │   ├── BranchIndicator  aria-hidden="true"
    │   │   └── BranchText
    │   └── BranchContent        role="group"
    │       └── (nested Branch or Leaf nodes)
    └── Leaf                       role="treeitem" (aria-expanded when has_children)
        └── LeafText             (<a> when href present)
```

| Part              | Element                               | Key Attributes                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Root`            | `<ul>` or `<div>`                     | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="root"`, `role="tree"`, `aria-multiselectable`                                                                                                                 |
| `Branch`          | `<li>` or `<div>`                     | `role="treeitem"`, `aria-expanded` (also set when `has_children` is true with no loaded children), `aria-selected`, `aria-level`, `aria-setsize`, `aria-posinset`, `data-ars-expanded`, `data-ars-selected` |
| `BranchControl`   | `<div>` or `<a>` (when href)          | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="branch-control"`, `href` (when present)                                                                                                                       |
| `BranchIndicator` | `<span>`                              | `data-ars-scope="tree-view"`, `data-ars-part="branch-indicator"`, `aria-hidden="true"`, `data-ars-expanded`                                                                                                 |

content.text: Now §3.5 — note the `ScrollFocusedIntoView` effect. Let me read it.

content.content:
1051	
1052	### 3.6 Expand/Collapse Keyboard Navigation Race Conditions
1053	
1054	Rapid keyboard interactions on `TreeView` nodes can cause race conditions between expand/collapse animations and focus movement:
1055	
1056	1. **Debounce Expansion**: Expand/collapse transitions are not debounced — each ArrowRight (expand) or ArrowLeft (collapse) takes effect immediately. However, the focus movement that follows expansion (ArrowRight on expanded node moves focus to first child) waits until the children are actually rendered.
1057	2. **Pending Expansion Guard**: If ArrowRight is pressed on a collapsed node, the node enters an "expanding" state. A second ArrowRight during this state is queued and executed after children render (moving focus to the first child), rather than being ignored.
1058	3. **Collapse During Focus-In-Child**: If ArrowLeft collapses a parent while focus is on a child, focus moves to the parent node before the collapse animation starts. The adapter must move focus synchronously, then animate the collapse.
1059	4. **Async Children**: For lazily-loaded children (`on_expand` triggers async fetch), the node shows a loading indicator and expansion completes when children arrive. ArrowRight during loading is a no-op.
1060

file.content:
### 3.6 Expand/Collapse Keyboard Navigation Race Conditions

Rapid keyboard interactions on `TreeView` nodes can cause race conditions between expand/collapse animations and focus movement:

1. **Debounce Expansion**: Expand/collapse transitions are not debounced — each ArrowRight (expand) or ArrowLeft (collapse) takes effect immediately. However, the focus movement that follows expansion (ArrowRight on expanded node moves focus to first child) waits until the children are actually rendered.
2. **Pending Expansion Guard**: If ArrowRight is pressed on a collapsed node, the node enters an "expanding" state. A second ArrowRight during this state is queued and executed after children render (moving focus to the first child), rather than being ignored.
3. **Collapse During Focus-In-Child**: If ArrowLeft collapses a parent while focus is on a child, focus moves to the parent node before the collapse animation starts. The adapter must move focus synchronously, then animate the collapse.
4. **Async Children**: For lazily-loaded children (`on_expand` triggers async fetch), the node shows a loading indicator and expansion completes when children arrive. ArrowRight during loading is a no-op.

1043	- Selected items have `aria-selected="true"` (already present)
1044	- Selection changes are announced via focus movement to the selected node
1045	- For programmatic selection changes (no focus change), use LiveAnnouncer:
1046	  `announce("{item label} selected", "polite")`
1047	
1048	### 3.5 Scroll Into View
1049	
1050	When focus moves to an off-screen tree item, the adapter scrolls it into view using `element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest', inline: 'nearest' })`. For nested scrollable containers, the adapter must walk up the DOM and call `scrollIntoView` on the closest scrollable ancestor only. On iOS Safari, where programmatic focus doesn't trigger scroll, the adapter must explicitly call `scrollIntoView` after `element.focus()`. Smooth scrolling is avoided for keyboard navigation to prevent timing conflicts.
1051

file.content:
- Selected items have `aria-selected="true"` (already present)
- Selection changes are announced via focus movement to the selected node
- For programmatic selection changes (no focus change), use LiveAnnouncer:
  `announce("{item label} selected", "polite")`

### 3.5 Scroll Into View

When focus moves to an off-screen tree item, the adapter scrolls it into view using `element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest', inline: 'nearest' })`. For nested scrollable containers, the adapter must walk up the DOM and call `scrollIntoView` on the closest scrollable ancestor only. On iOS Safari, where programmatic focus doesn't trigger scroll, the adapter must explicitly call `scrollIntoView` after `element.focus()`. Smooth scrolling is avoided for keyboard navigation to prevent timing conflicts.

### 3.5 Scroll Into View

When focus moves to an off-screen tree item, the adapter scrolls it into view using `element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest', inline: 'nearest' })`. For nested scrollable containers, the adapter must walk up the DOM and call `scrollIntoView` on the closest scrollable ancestor only. On iOS Safari, where programmatic focus doesn't trigger scroll, the adapter must explicitly call `scrollIntoView` after `element.focus()`. Smooth scrolling is avoided for keyboard navigation to prevent timing conflicts.
input.new_string:
### 3.5 Scroll Into View

The tree uses the active-descendant pattern: DOM focus stays on the `Root`
(`tabindex="0"`), and `aria-activedescendant` points at the focused node's id.
When focus moves, the agnostic core emits the `Effect::ScrollFocusedIntoView`
intent (on every focus-moving transition — `FocusNode`, `FocusNext`/`Prev`/
`First`/`Last`/`Parent`, and `TypeaheadSearch`); it never moves DOM focus or
scrolls itself.

The adapter resolves that intent: it scrolls the active-descendant element into view using `element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest', inline: 'nearest' })`. For nested scrollable containers, the adapter must walk up the DOM and call `scrollIntoView` on the closest scrollable ancestor only. On iOS Safari, where programmatic focus doesn't trigger scroll, the adapter must explicitly call `scrollIntoView`. Smooth scrolling is avoided for keyboard navigation to prevent timing conflicts.

content.text: The `spec-compile-snippets` gate compiles ` ```rust ` blocks (excluding `no_check`). My blocks reference types defined across sibling blocks, so I need to understand how it assembles them. Let me read the harness.

content.content:
1	//! `spec compile-snippets` — parse Rust code blocks in spec markdown
2	//! through `syn::parse_file` and surface syntax errors.
3	//!
4	//! ## What this catches
5	//!
6	//! - Top-level Rust syntax bugs in spec snippets: missing braces,
7	//!   mistyped `pub fn`, unterminated string literals, unbalanced
8	//!   generics outside macro bodies, etc.
9	//! - Coverage across the full spec corpus —
10	//!   `spec/{components,foundation,leptos-components,dioxus-components,shared,testing}/`.
11	//!   The pre-existing `lint-code` linter only walked
12	//!   `spec/components/` and never invoked a Rust parser, so foundation
13	//!   and per-adapter specs had zero parse coverage.
14	//!
15	//! ## What this **does not** catch (important — read this)
16	//!
17	//! - **Macro-internal syntax.** `view! { … }` and `rsx! { … }` are
18	//!   opaque token trees to both `syn` and `rustc`; everything inside
19	//!   the outer braces is unchecked. The original Leptos `view!`
20	//!   regression (an inline `|errors: ArcRwSignal<Errors>|` closure
21	//!   where the macro parser misread `<Errors>` as an HTML tag) sits
22	//!   *inside* the macro and would not be caught here. The unit test
23	//!   suites at `crates/ars-{leptos,dioxus}/tests/` are the source of
24	//!   truth for macro-internal correctness.
25	//! - **Type / path resolution.** A snippet that names a type defined
26	//!   nowhere in the workspace still parses syntactically. Use
27	//!   `cargo check` against a fixture crate for that.
28	//! - **Borrow-checker / ownership errors.** Same as above —
29	//!   `syn::parse_file` is purely a parser.
30	//!
31	//! ## Opting out
32	//!
33	//! Code blocks that intentionally contain partial or pseudo-Rust
34	//! (signatures shown for documentation purposes, top-level expressions
35	//! meant to live inside an outer fn body, etc.) opt out by tagging the
36	//! fence info-string with `rust,no_check` (also recognised:
37	//! `rust,ignore`, `rust,no_run`, mirroring rustdoc). The extractor
38	//! honors those markers and skips the block.
39	//!
40	//! ## Performance
41	//!
42	//! Fast: no compiler invocation. Runs in milliseconds across all 2000+
43	//! Rust blocks in the spec, so it is suitable for inclusion in the
44	//! `cargo xci` pipeline (see `xtask::ci::Step::SpecCompileSnippets`).
45	
46	use std::{fs, path::Path};
47	
48	use crate::manifest::{Error, SpecRoot};
49	
50	/// CLI entry point.
51	///
52	/// Always returns `Ok(String)`. The text either begins with `ok:` (no
53	/// findings) or with `N finding(s):` followed by per-finding lines.
54	///
55	/// When `fix` is `true`, each failing block has its opening fence rewritten
56	/// from `` ```rust `` (or `` ```rs ``) to `` ```rust,no_check `` (or
57	/// `` ```rs,no_check ``) in place; the file is rewritten on disk and the
58	/// returned text reports how many fences were patched.
59	///
60	/// # Errors
61	///
62	/// Returns [`Error::Io`] when a target file cannot be read or written.
63	pub fn execute(root: &SpecRoot, fix: bool) -> Result<String, Error> {
64	    let mut files = Vec::new();
65	
66	    for sub in [
67	        "components",
68	        "foundation",
69	        "leptos-components",
70	        "dioxus-components",
71	        "shared",
72	        "testing",
73	    ] {
74	        let dir = root.path.join(sub);
75	
76	        collect_md_files(&dir, &mut files).map_err(Error::Io)?;
77	    }
78	
79	    files.sort();
80	
81	    let display_root = fs::canonicalize(&root.path).unwrap_or_else(|_| root.path.clone());
82	
83	    let mut findings = Vec::new();
84	    let mut blocks_checked = 0usize;
85	    let mut fences_patched = 0usize;
86	
87	    for path in &files {
88	        let source = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(Error::Io)?;
89	
90	        let display = path
91	            .strip_prefix(&display_root)
92	            .unwrap_or(path)
93	            .display()
94	            .to_string();
95	
96	        let blocks = extract_rust_blocks(&source);
97	
98	        let mut failing_fence_lines = Vec::new();
99	
100	        for block in &blocks {
101	            blocks_checked += 1;
102	
103	            if let Some(message) = check_block(block) {
104	                findings.push(Finding {
105	                    file: display.clone(),
106	                    line: block.start_line,
107	                    message,
108	                });
109	
110	                if fix {
111	                    // The fence line is one above the block's first content line.
112	                    failing_fence_lines.push(block.start_line.saturating_sub(1));
113	                }
114	            }
115	        }
116	
117	        if fix && !failing_fence_lines.is_empty() {
118	            let patched = rewrite_fences_to_no_check(&source, &failing_fence_lines);
119	
120	            if patched != source {
121	                let count = patched_fence_count(&source, &patched);
122	
123	                fences_patched += count;
124	
125	                fs::write(path, patched).map_err(Error::Io)?;
126	            }
127	        }
128	    }
129	
130	    let mut out = String::new();
